sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 21.58 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.21)

statement.latex: We say that a product$XY=\{xy x X,\;y Y\}$of two subsets$X,Y$of a group$G$is if for every$z XY$there are unique$x X$,$y Y$such that$z=xy$. Is there an infinite group$G$such that every subset$A G$satisfies the following property: all the maximal subsets$B$for which the product$AB$is direct have the same cardinality?
